title: region — declared entitlements
description: Three entitlement keys. `region_slot` gates the quantity of Regions per tenant (matches hierarchy.md §7 tier matrix). `region_custom_tax_rules` gates the multi-rate tax_config.rates[] array. `region_time_travel` gates retroactive tax_config edits — Enterprise-only + heavily audited.

entitlements.description: Max Regions per tenant. Matches `hierarchy.md` §7: Small=1 (default-only), Medium up to 3, Enterprise=unlimited (null). Consumed on create. The SYSTEM-provisioned default Region (via SeedDefaultRegionForTenantJob) does NOT consume a slot — it's a platform invariant. Refuses further Region creates with `RegionQuotaExceeded` (402) once the cap is reached. The observer decrements on create + returns the slot on hard-delete (soft-delete keeps the slot occupied per financial retention). Downgrading a Medium tenant back to Small does NOT auto-archive extra Regions — the entitlement reconciler surfaces the drift + suggests a manual archive path.

Enables multi-rate tax_config with the `rates[]` array — per-category (e.g. reduced VAT for children's programmes, luxury tax, alcohol tax). Off = only `tax_config.default_rate_percent` + `name` + `inclusive` are honoured; any `rates[]` entries in the payload are refused with `REGION_CUSTOM_TAX_RULES_DISABLED` (402). Enterprise-only because multi-rate jurisdictions add invoice-generation complexity that most tenants don't need.

Enables RETROACTIVE tax_config edits that replay against already-issued Invoices. Enterprise-only + off by default even for Enterprise (a per-tenant activation gate requires ops sign-off + a compliance letter). When off, tax_config changes affect ONLY future invoices — historical rows keep the snapshot they were issued with. Wave 3 feature; the payload contract is documented here but the code lives behind the feature flag `region.time_travel_execution` until Wave 3 lands.
